name: Adolf Fick
burial: Blankenberge, Belgium (died there; specific cemetery unconfirmed)

name: Albert Einstein
burial: Cremated; ashes scattered in Delaware River (secret location)
eponymous_terms: Einstein (E); General relativity; Special relativity; Photoelectric effect; Bose-Einstein condensate; Einstein solid
fields: Gravitation; Photochemistry; Quantum mechanics; Relativity; Solid state physics
notes: Cremated same day he died to prevent grave becoming pilgrimage site. Brain removed during autopsy; portions now at Mütter Museum, Philadelphia.

name: Alessandro Volta
burial: Camnago Volta Cemetery, Camnago Volta, Como, Italy
notes: The village was renamed "Camnago Volta" in his honor. Neoclassical mausoleum completed in 1831.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
burial: Beinn Bhreagh Estate (mountaintop), Baddeck, Nova Scotia, Canada
notes: Private grave on mountain peak overlooking Bras d'Or Lake. Not open to public.

name: Amedeo Avogadro
notes: Full name: Lorenzo Romano Amedeo Carlo Avogadro, Conte di Quaregna e di Cerreto.

name: Anders Celsius
burial: Gamla Uppsala kyrka (Old Uppsala Church), Uppsala, Sweden
notes: Buried beneath the church floor near ancient royal mounds. Died of tuberculosis at age 42.

name: Anders Jonas Ångström
burial: Uppsala gamla kyrkogård (Uppsala Old Cemetery), Uppsala, Sweden
notes: Black polished granite tomb erected in 1883.

name: André-Marie Ampère
notes: Initially buried in Marseille; remains later transferred to Paris alongside his son Jean-Jacques Ampère.

name: Antoine Baumé
notes: Invented the Baumé scale hydrometer. Lost fortune during French Revolution.

name: Archimedes
notes: Killed by Roman soldier during siege of Syracuse. Cicero found the overgrown tomb in 75 BC, but it has been lost since antiquity.

name: Arnold Sommerfeld
notes: Killed in traffic accident while walking with grandchildren; struck by truck he didn't hear due to near-deafness.

name: Arthur Compton
burial: Wooster Cemetery, Wooster, Ohio, United States
notes: Though he died in Berkeley, California, he was returned to his hometown.

name: August Beer
notes: Died at age 38.

name: Augustin Fresnel
notes: Died of tuberculosis at age 39.

name: Blaise Pascal
notes: Grave suffered some desecration during the French Revolution in 1793.

name: Brian Josephson
notes: Nobel Prize 1973. Emeritus Professor at Cambridge; directs Mind-Matter Unification Project.

name: C.V. Raman
burial: Cremated in Bangalore (Bengaluru), Karnataka, India (ashes scattered)
notes: First Asian Nobel laureate in Physics (1930). Requested "simple cremation, no mumbo-jumbo."

name: Carl Friedrich Gauss
eponymous_terms: Gauss (G); Gauss's law; Gauss's law for magnetism
notes: Cemetery now part of Cheltenhampark. Grave frequently visited.

name: Charles-Augustin de Coulomb
burial: Paris, France (specific cemetery not definitively documented)
notes: His name is inscribed on the Eiffel Tower.

name: Christiaan Huygens
burial: Grote of Sint-Jacobskerk (St. James Church), The Hague, Netherlands (unmarked grave)

name: Christian Doppler
burial: Cimitero di San Michele, Venice, Italy
notes: Died of tuberculosis. Venice was part of Austrian Empire at the time.

name: Daniel Bernoulli
notes: Family moved to Basel when he was 5 years old.

name: David Bohm
notes: Died in London of heart failure.

name: David Brewster
burial: Melrose Abbey, Melrose, Scotland, United Kingdom

name: Edwin Hall
birthplace: Great Falls (later North Gorham), Maine, United States
burial: Mount Auburn Cemetery, Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States

name: Edwin Hubble
notes: His wife Grace never revealed where his remains were buried or scattered. No funeral was held.

name: Emmy Noether
burial: The Cloisters, Bryn Mawr College, Pennsylvania, United States
notes: Cremated; urn buried under inconspicuous cement slab marked only "E.N."

name: Enrico Fermi
burial: Oak Woods Cemetery, Chicago, Illinois, United States
eponymous_terms: Fermi (fm); Fermion; Fermi gas; Fermi surface; Fermi energy
fields: Condensed matter; Nuclear physics; Particle physics; Solid state physics; Statistical mechanics
notes: Died of stomach cancer. Remains in Chicago despite efforts to transfer to Florence.

name: Ernest Rutherford
burial: Westminster Abbey (nave, ashes interred), London, United Kingdom
notes: Ashes buried near Newton and Kelvin. "Father of nuclear physics."

name: Erwin Schrödinger
burial: Alpbach Graveyard (St. Oswald's Church Cemetery), Alpbach, Tyrol, Austria
notes: Though not Catholic, buried in Catholic cemetery as a member of Pontifical Academy of Sciences. Gravestone bears his wave equation.

name: Ettore Majorana
notes: Vanished March 25, 1938 after boarding ship from Palermo to Naples. One of physics' greatest mysteries. Italian court declared presumed death in January 2025. Investigators believe he may have lived in Venezuela under alias "Bini" from 1955-1959.

name: Evangelista Torricelli
burial: Basilica di San Lorenzo, Florence, Italy
notes: Died of typhoid fever at age 39.

name: Frank Drake
burial: Details not publicly available (died in Aptos, California)
notes: Creator of the Drake Equation for estimating extraterrestrial civilizations.

name: Friedrich Hund
notes: Lived to age **101**, one of the longest-lived physicists.

name: Friedrich Pockels
burial: Heidelberg, Germany (grave photo exists; cemetery name not confirmed)

name: Galileo Galilei
burial: Basilica di Santa Croce, Florence, Italy
notes: Initially buried secretly in 1642 due to heresy conviction; moved to monumental tomb in 1737. Three fingers, a tooth, and a vertebra removed as relics.

name: Gaspard-Gustave de Coriolis
notes: Name on Eiffel Tower.

name: Georg Simon Ohm
burial: Alter Südfriedhof (Old South Cemetery), Munich, Germany
notes: Died after delivering a lecture; birthdate on gravestone incorrectly states 1787.

name: George Stokes
burial: Mill Road Cemetery, Cambridge, United Kingdom
notes: No visible grave marker; location documented in Sexton's grave book.

name: Gerhard Borrmann
notes: Refused to join Nazi Party in 1938.

name: Germain Hess
burial: Smolensky Lutheran Cemetery, Saint Petersburg, Russia
notes: Family moved to Russia when he was 3. Known there as German Ivanovich Gess.

name: Guglielmo Marconi
burial: Mausoleum of Guglielmo Marconi, Sasso Marconi (formerly Pontecchio Marconi), Italy
notes: Town renamed in his honor in 1938.

name: Gustav Kirchhoff
eponymous_terms: Kirchhoff's circuit laws; Kirchhoff's law of radiation
notes: Grave near the Brothers Grimm and Leopold Kronecker.

name: Gustav Mie
burial: Location unknown (died in Freiburg im Breisgau)

name: Hans Christian Ørsted
notes: Same cemetery as Hans Christian Andersen and Søren Kierkegaard.

name: Heike Kamerlingh Onnes
burial: Nederlands Hervormd Kerkhof (Dorpskerk Voorschoten), Voorschoten, Netherlands
notes: Nobel Prize 1913 for liquefying helium.

name: Heinrich Lenz
notes: Baltic German origin. Traveled to Italy for medical treatment and died there.

name: Heinrich Magnus
notes: Same cemetery as Hegel, Fichte, and Brecht.

name: Heinrich Rudolf Hertz
notes: Died at age 36 in Bonn; remains returned to his hometown.

name: Hendrik Lorentz
notes: Funeral attended by Einstein, Marie Curie, and Rutherford. Dutch telegraph services suspended for three minutes during funeral.

name: Henri Becquerel
burial: Villa Pen-Castel (family property), Le Croisic, Loire-Atlantique, France
notes: Nobel Prize winner 1903 for discovering radioactivity. Died at his vacation home.

name: Henry Darcy
burial: Cimetière de Dijon (formerly Cimetière de Péjoces), Dijon, France
notes: Died of pneumonia while traveling. Day after burial, main square renamed "Place Darcy."

name: Hermann Weyl
burial: Princeton Cemetery, Princeton, New Jersey, United States
notes: Died unexpectedly in Zürich while mailing a letter. Buried in Princeton where he worked at IAS.

name: Hermann von Helmholtz
burial: Friedhof Wannsee II (Lindenstraße), Wannsee, Berlin, Germany

name: Irving Langmuir
notes: Died at nephew's summer home in Woods Hole, Massachusetts.

name: Isaac Newton
burial: Westminster Abbey (nave), London, England, United Kingdom
eponymous_terms: Newton (N); Newton's laws of motion; Law of universal gravitation
notes: First scientist buried in Westminster Abbey. Monument completed 1731; Latin epitaph reads "Here lies that which was mortal of Isaac Newton."

name: J.J. Thomson
birthplace: Cheetham Hill, Manchester, England, United Kingdom
burial: Westminster Abbey (ashes interred in nave), London, United Kingdom
notes: Ashes near Newton and Rutherford.

name: Jacob Bekenstein
notes: Died in Helsinki from heart attack. As a religious Jew, buried in Israel per tradition.

name: James Clerk Maxwell
burial: Parton Churchyard (Old Kirk ruins), Parton, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotland, United Kingdom
notes: Buried with parents and wife in church ruins near family estate at Glenlair. Memorial stone at Westminster Abbey.

name: James Early
burial: Location not documented (died in Palo Alto, California)
notes: Known for the Early effect in transistors.

name: James Prescott Joule
birthplace: Salford, Greater Manchester, England, United Kingdom
burial: Brooklands Cemetery, Sale, Greater Manchester, England, United Kingdom
eponymous_terms: Joule (J); Joule's first law; Joule-Thomson effect
notes: Gravestone inscribed with "772.55"—his determination of the mechanical equivalent of heat. Not buried in Westminster Abbey despite a memorial plaque there.

name: James Watt
burial: St. Mary's Church, Handsworth, Birmingham, England, United Kingdom
notes: Church expansion over his grave means the tomb is now located inside the church building.

name: Jean Peltier
notes: Originally a watchmaker; became self-taught physicist after retiring at age 30.

name: Jean Poiseuille
notes: Known for the Hagen-Poiseuille equation.

name: Jean d'Alembert
notes: Born illegitimate and abandoned on church steps. As a known atheist, denied proper burial.

name: Jean-Victor Poncelet
notes: Founded modern projective geometry while imprisoned in Russia.

name: Johann Heinrich Lambert
notes: Died of tuberculosis.

name: Johannes Kepler
burial: GRAVE DESTROYED (originally St. Peter's Cemetery, Regensburg, Germany)
notes: Grave destroyed when Swedish troops sieged Regensburg in 1632, just two years after his death.

name: Johannes Rydberg
notes: Died at Lund Hospital.

name: Johannes Stark
birthplace: Schickenhof (now part of Freihung), Bavaria, Germany
notes: Nobel Prize 1919. Nazi supporter; sentenced as "Major Offender" in 1947.

name: Johannes van der Waals
burial: De Nieuwe Ooster Cemetery, Amsterdam, Netherlands
eponymous_terms: Van der Waals force; Van der Waals equation

name: John Dalton
birthplace: Eaglesfield, near Cockermouth, Cumberland, United Kingdom
notes: Cemetery now a playing field, but grave survives. Over 40,000 people attended his funeral.

name: John Hopkinson
burial: Cimetière de Territet, Territet (near Montreux), Switzerland
notes: Died in mountaineering accident with three of his children.

name: John Kerr
notes: Ordained minister who worked at Free Church Training College for 44 years.

name: John Napier
burial: St Cuthbert's Parish Church (underground vault), Edinburgh, United Kingdom
notes: Remains transferred from St Giles kirkyard when it was demolished for Parliament House.

name: John Stewart Bell
notes: Died unexpectedly of cerebral hemorrhage.

name: John Tyndall
burial: St. Bartholomew's Churchyard, Haslemere, Surrey, England, United Kingdom
notes: Died from accidental overdose of chloral hydrate administered by his wife.

name: John William Strutt, Lord Rayleigh
birthplace: Langford Grove, Maldon, Essex, United Kingdom
burial: All Saints' Church graveyard, Terling, Essex, United Kingdom
notes: Buried in parish churchyard of family estate at Terling Place.

name: Josef Stefan
birthplace: St. Peter (Sveti Peter), near Klagenfurt, Austria
burial: Zentralfriedhof, Vienna, Austria (grave has no visible marker)
notes: Parents were ethnic Slovenes. Born illegitimate.

name: Joseph Fourier
notes: Grave features Egyptian-style decoration reflecting his time during Napoleon's Egyptian expedition.

name: Joseph Henry
burial: Oak Hill Cemetery, Washington, D.C., United States
notes: Funeral attended by President Rutherford B. Hayes. First Secretary of the Smithsonian Institution.

name: Joseph Larmor
birthplace: Magheragall, County Antrim, Ireland (now Northern Ireland)
burial: Holywood Priory Cemetery, Holywood, County Down, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom

name: Joseph-Louis Lagrange
notes: Born Giuseppe Lodovico Lagrangia. Napoleon honored him two days before his death.

name: Joseph-Nicolas Delisle
notes: Died at Abbey of St. Genevieve. Invented the Delisle temperature scale.

name: Josiah Willard Gibbs
burial: Grove Street Cemetery, New Haven, Connecticut, United States
notes: Born and buried in same city where he spent entire career at Yale.

name: Julian Schwinger
burial: Mount Auburn Cemetery (Willow Pond Knoll Garden), Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States
notes: Tombstone features his calculation of correction to magnetic moment of electron.

name: Karl Jansky
birthplace: Norman, Oklahoma Territory (now Oklahoma), United States
burial: Forest Hill Cemetery, Madison, Wisconsin, United States
notes: Born when Oklahoma was still a territory. Died of heart failure at age 44.

name: Karl Schwarzschild
notes: Died of pemphigus contracted while serving on WWI Russian front at age 42.

name: Klaus von Klitzing
eponymous_terms: Von Klitzing constant (RK); Quantum Hall effect
notes: Nobel Prize 1985. Director Emeritus at Max Planck Institute for Solid State Research.

name: Leon Cooper
birthplace: The Bronx, New York, United States
burial: Details not yet publicly documented (died October 2024 in Providence, Rhode Island)
notes: Nobel Prize 1972 for BCS theory of superconductivity.

name: Leonhard Euler
burial: Lazarevskoe Cemetery, Alexander Nevsky Monastery, St. Petersburg, Russia
notes: Originally buried at Smolensk Lutheran Cemetery; remains reinterred in 1956 for 250th anniversary.

name: Lise Meitner
burial: St James's Churchyard, Bramley, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom
notes: Epitaph reads: "A physicist who never lost her humanity." Element 109 (Meitnerium) named in her honor.

name: Louis Harold Gray
notes: Founded radiobiology field at Mount Vernon Hospital. Specific burial documentation unavailable.

name: Ludwig Boltzmann
eponymous_terms: Boltzmann constant (kB); Stefan-Boltzmann constant (Ïƒ); Stefan-Boltzmann law; Boltzmann equation
notes: Died by suicide in Duino (now Italy). Tombstone features his entropy equation **S = k log W**.

name: Léon Brillouin
burial: Location unknown (died in New York City)
notes: U.S. citizen from 1949.

name: Marie Curie
notes: First woman interred in the Panthéon on her own merits (1995 transfer). Coffin lined with lead due to radiation.

name: Max Born
notes: Cemetery contains graves of nine Nobel laureates. Gravestone displays quantum mechanics formula.

name: Max Planck
notes: Simple gravestone inscribed only with his name and **Planck's constant value** (h=6.62×10⁻³⁴ W·s²).

name: Meghnad Saha
burial: Cremated at Keoratola Crematorium, Kolkata, India
notes: Requested "simple cremation without any rituals."

name: Michael Faraday
birthplace: Newington Butts, Surrey (now London Borough of Southwark), England, United Kingdom
burial: Highgate Cemetery (West Cemetery), London, England, United Kingdom
eponymous_terms: Farad (F); Faraday (Fd); Faraday constant (F); Faraday's law of induction; Faraday's laws of electrolysis; Faraday effect
notes: Declined burial in Westminster Abbey. Grave is Grade II listed; buried in Sandemanian plot with his wife.

name: Nicolas Sadi Carnot
notes: Died of cholera at age 36. Many belongings and writings buried with him due to contamination fears. "Father of Thermodynamics."

name: Niels Bohr
notes: Cremated; buried in family grave with father Christian and brother Harald. Grave features granite pillar topped with an owl.

name: Nikola Tesla
burial: Nikola Tesla Museum, Belgrade, Serbia (cremated; ashes in golden spherical urn)
notes: Cremated in New York; ashes kept at Ferncliff Cemetery until 1957, then transferred to Belgrade.

name: Ole Rømer
burial: Copenhagen, Denmark (likely Vor Frue Kirke; exact location uncertain)
notes: First to demonstrate light has finite speed (1676). Written work lost in Great Copenhagen Fire of 1728.

name: Osborne Reynolds
birthplace: Belfast, Ireland (now Northern Ireland, United Kingdom)
burial: St Decuman's Churchyard, Watchet, Somerset, United Kingdom
notes: Died of influenza.

name: Paul Dirac
burial: Roselawn Cemetery, Tallahassee, Florida, United States
eponymous_terms: Reduced Planck constant (â„); Dirac fermion; Dirac equation
notes: Memorial stone with Dirac equation unveiled at Westminster Abbey in 1995.

name: Paul Ehrenfest
notes: Died by suicide. Some sources mention Leiden; unverified.

name: Pavel Cherenkov
notes: Nobel Prize 1958. Novodevichy is Russia's most prestigious cemetery.

name: Peter Debye
burial: Pleasant Grove Cemetery, Ithaca, New York, United States
notes: U.S. citizen from 1946. Died of a heart attack.

name: Peter Higgs
birthplace: Newcastle upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
burial: Details not made public (died in Edinburgh)
notes: Nobel Prize 2013. Died peacefully at home following short illness.

name: Pierre Auger
notes: First Director General of ESRO (precursor to ESA).

name: Pierre Curie
notes: Reinterred with Marie in 1995 from Cimetière de Sceaux. Killed at age 46 in a street accident.

name: Pierre de Fermat
burial: Church of the Augustines, Toulouse, France (transferred 1675)
notes: Originally buried at Church of St. Dominique, Castres; remains moved to family vault.

name: Pierre-Louis Maupertuis
notes: Died in Basel while staying with Johann Bernoulli; remains later transferred to Paris.

name: Pierre-Simon Laplace
burial: Pierre-Simon Laplace Estate Cemetery, Saint-Julien-de-Mailloc, France
notes: Originally in Père Lachaise; transferred to family estate in 1888.

name: Pieter Zeeman
notes: Nobel Prize 1902.

name: René de Réaumur
notes: Died from injuries after falling from his horse.

name: Richard Feynman
birthplace: Far Rockaway, Queens, New York, United States
burial: Mountain View Cemetery, Altadena, California, United States
notes: Simple plaque marks grave. Nobel Prize 1965.

name: Robert Boyle
burial: St Martin-in-the-Fields Churchyard, Westminster, London, United Kingdom

name: Robert Hooke
birthplace: Freshwater, Isle of Wight, England, United Kingdom
burial: Original grave lost (originally St Helen's Church, Bishopsgate, London)
notes: In 1891, remains exhumed and transferred to common grave at City of London Cemetery, Wanstead. Precise location unknown.

name: Rolf Sievert
burial: Location uncertain (died at Karolinska Hospital, Stockholm)
notes: Known as "Father of Radiation Protection." Specific cemetery documentation not found.

name: Rudolf Mössbauer
burial: Waldfriedhof Grünwald, Grünwald, near Munich, Germany
notes: Nobel Prize 1961.

name: Satyendra Nath Bose
notes: Bosons named after him.

name: Siméon Denis Poisson
notes: Elevated to Baron in 1821 but never used the title.

name: Sin-Itiro Tomonaga
burial: Tama Cemetery (Tama Reien), Fuchu City, Tokyo, Japan
notes: Nobel Prize winner. Grave near Yoshio Nishina, "Father of Japanese Nuclear Physics."

name: Stephen Hawking
burial: Westminster Abbey (ashes interred in Scientists' Corner), London, United Kingdom
notes: Ashes placed between Newton and Darwin. Memorial stone bears his entropy equation.

name: Subrahmanyan Chandrasekhar
notes: Nobel Prize 1983. Died in Chicago of heart attack.

name: Svante Arrhenius
burial: Uppsala Old Cemetery (Uppsala Gamla kyrkogård), Uppsala, Sweden
notes: Died in Stockholm but buried in Uppsala as he wished.

name: Theodore von Kármán
burial: Hollywood Forever Cemetery (Beth Olam Mausoleum), Los Angeles, California, United States
notes: Died in Aachen visiting friends; body flown to California. First National Medal of Science recipient.

name: Thomas Graham
burial: Glasgow Cathedral burial ground, Glasgow, Scotland, United Kingdom
notes: "Father of colloid chemistry." Body returned to Glasgow from London.

name: Thomas Young
burial: St. Giles' Church Cemetery, Farnborough, Kent, England, United Kingdom
notes: Memorial tablet at Westminster Abbey, but body buried at Farnborough.

name: Walter Schottky
burial: Location not documented (died in Pretzfeld, Germany)

name: Walther Meissner
notes: Spent final years alone with his dogs.

name: Walther Nernst
notes: Buried near Planck, Hahn, and von Laue. Nobel Prize 1920 (Chemistry).

name: Werner Heisenberg
burial: Waldfriedhof München (Munich Forest Cemetery), Munich, Germany
notes: Died of kidney cancer.

name: Werner von Siemens
birthplace: Lenthe (now part of Gehrden), Lower Saxony, Germany
notes: Raised to nobility in 1888 (adding "von" to his name). Died of pneumonia.

name: Wilhelm Eduard Weber
notes: One of the "Göttingen Seven" dismissed in 1837 for protesting constitutional changes.

name: Wilhelm Röntgen
notes: First Nobel Prize in Physics recipient (1901). Buried with parents per his wishes.

name: Wilhelm Wien
birthplace: Gaffken, East Prussia (now Parusnoye, Kaliningrad Oblast, Russia)
burial: Waldfriedhof München (Munich Forest Cemetery), Munich, Germany
eponymous_terms: Wien displacement constant (b); Wien's displacement law
notes: Birthplace now in Russian territory.

name: Willebrord Snell
notes: Twenty students carried his coffin.

name: William Gilbert
burial: Holy Trinity Church, Colchester, England, United Kingdom
notes: Died of bubonic plague during 1603 epidemic. Personal physician to Queen Elizabeth I.

name: William Henry
burial: Cross Street Unitarian Chapel burial ground, Manchester, United Kingdom
notes: Took his own life due to chronic ill health.

name: William Lawrence Bragg
burial: Trinity College Chapel, Cambridge, England, United Kingdom
notes: Youngest-ever Nobel laureate in physics (age 25, shared with his father).

name: William Thomson, Lord Kelvin
burial: Westminster Abbey (nave), London, England, United Kingdom
notes: Interred near Isaac Newton's grave; a commemorative stained glass window was erected in 1913.

name: Wolfgang Pauli
notes: Hospital room number was **137**, significant as the fine-structure constant approximates 1/137.

name: Yakir Aharonov
birthplace: Haifa, British Mandate of Palestine (now Israel)
notes: Elected to Royal Society in June 2024. Professor at Chapman University.
